methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Transportation. Range: $270M - $1.8B.

data.narrative:
The ePlane Company is a Series B transportation-sector venture that has raised $14M+ and is currently fundraising for $40–50M. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of eight private companies in the same sector and stage vicinity, including Einride (Series C, $1.8B), Harbinger (Series C, $500M), and REGENT Craft (Series A, $270M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, which uses the $14M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applies a 1.9x Series B-to-next-stage multiplier, implies a valuation of $27M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $500M, with a range spanning $270M to $1.8B. The stage-progression method captures recent capital history and typical venture step-ups; the peer-median method anchors to market outcomes but reflects substantial heterogeneity in the comp set.

Triangulating both methods produces a range of $23M (low) to $575M (high), with a median of $263M. The wide dispersion—spanning nearly two orders of magnitude—reflects material uncertainty in how frontier transportation ventures are currently valued. Key drivers of this uncertainty include the relative stage positioning of peers (ranging from Series A to Series C), the nascency of the eVTOL subsector, and the absence of public market comparables. The median estimate of $263M is most defensible if ePlane's competitive position and technology maturity align with mid-stage peers like Harbinger; the low estimate applies only under conservative stage-based growth assumptions.
